title: Adaptive Agent Routing with Capability-Based Selection
source: LangGraph supervisor pattern + CrewAI 0.80+ skill-based routing (2025-2026 best practices for multi-agent orchestration)
description: The current auto_select_agent() in delegate.py uses simple keyword matching against ticket title/description to pick an agent. Modern frameworks like LangGraph and CrewAI use capability-based routing where agents declare structured capabilities (tools, languages, domains) and a lightweight LLM call scores agent-task fit. This eliminates keyword-list maintenance and handles ambiguous tickets (e.g. 'add authenticated GraphQL endpoint' which scores for backend, security, and architect). A small Claude Haiku call scoring each candidate agent's capability overlap against the ticket would be far more accurate and self-maintaining.
proposed_changes: 1. Add a `capabilities` field to each agent JSON file (e.g. backend-morty.json gets `{"capabilities": ["python", "node", "rest-api", "graphql", "database", "migrations"]}`). 2. Replace the keyword-matching `auto_select_agent()` in delegate.py with a function that loads all agent capability manifests, constructs a short prompt like 'Given this ticket [title+desc], score each agent 1-10 for fit: [agent: capabilities]', calls `claude -p --model haiku` to get a JSON score map, and picks the highest scorer. 3. Add a fallback to the current keyword approach if the Haiku call fails. This mirrors LangGraph's supervisor-with-tools pattern where the supervisor LLM picks which specialist to invoke.
